findings.message: session.commit() failure produces an unhandled 500 with no structured log at the call site. The item_id context is lost; only the global exception handler fires, without mutation-specific fields. This is a new unmetered/unlogged error path on a mutating endpoint.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the commit+refresh block in try/except, log the exception with item_id (and correlation_id) at ERROR level, then re-raise or convert to an HTTPException(500) so the call site log entry is emitted regardless of global handler behavior.

findings.message: _log.warning('archive_item_not_found') logs item_id but no correlation_id. If the structlog pipeline does not auto-bind a request correlation ID via contextvars middleware, this 404 error event cannot be correlated to its originating trace or request in log aggregation.
findings.suggestion: Confirm that logging_config.get_logger() configures a contextvars processor that injects the correlation_id (e.g. from the ASGI scope or OTel trace context) automatically. If not, pass it explicitly: _log.warning('archive_item_not_found', item_id=item_id, correlation_id=...).

findings.message: _log.info('archive_item_completed') has the same missing correlation_id as the 404 path — successful mutation events are also unlinked from their originating request in log aggregation, defeating end-to-end trace correlation for the happy path.
findings.suggestion: Same fix as line 51: ensure the structlog context processor binds correlation_id for every request, or pass it explicitly here.

findings.message: No SLO threshold is defined in slo.py / default_slos() for the new POST /items/{item_id}/archive endpoint. The global request_latency_p99_ms and error_rate_pct SLOs apply fleet-wide but cannot surface archive-specific degradation (e.g. elevated 404 or commit-failure rates on this path).
findings.suggestion: Add a dedicated Prometheus counter (e.g. items_archived_total / items_archive_errors_total) emitted at lines 52 and 54, and consider an endpoint-scoped SLO entry or Grafana panel so archive failures can be alerted on independently of the global error rate.

findings.message: session.refresh() executes after a successful commit. If refresh raises (e.g. row deleted by a concurrent transaction between commit and refresh), the archive mutation DID succeed but archive_item_completed is never logged — creating a silent observability gap where the operation is unrecorded despite having taken effect.
findings.suggestion: Emit _log.info('archive_item_committed', item_id=item_id) immediately after session.commit() to anchor durability in logs unconditionally, keeping the existing archive_item_completed log after refresh as a secondary response-ready event.
